sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम्।
सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
dṛṣṭvemaṃ svajanaṃ kṛṣṇa yuyutsuṃ samupasthitam
sīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhaṃ ca pariśuṣyati
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: O Krishna, seeing these kinsmen arrayed and eager to fight, my limbs sink and my mouth dries up.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s body speaks the first truth of trauma: when the mind confronts an irreconcilable conflict—having to harm what one loves—it dissociates; vitality drains, language dries, and the self fragments. This moment foreshadows the universal human crisis of moral injury, where duty demands betrayal of attachment, and the nervous system collapses under the weight of impossible choice.
psychological_state: A high-functioning individual whose body suddenly betrays them when forced to choose between competing loyalties, revealing the unconscious truth that love is stronger than ideology.
trigger_scenario: A manager ordered to lay off lifelong friends during a corporate downsizing feels her legs buckle and her throat close as she reads their names on the termination list.
anchor_text: When love and duty collide, the body speaks the unspeakable.
